title: Safe and Affordable Drugs from Canada Act of 2025
short_title: Safe and Affordable Drugs from Canada Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill would let Americans buy prescription drugs from Canada for their personal use. The government would check and approve certain Canadian pharmacies that meet safety rules, then put those pharmacies on a list that people can find online. Americans could only buy a 90-day supply at a time using a prescription from their American doctor, and the Canadian medicine would have to be the same as what is already approved in the United States. Some types of drugs like controlled substances and certain injected medicines would not be allowed to be imported from Canada.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions.
subjects: Department of Health and Human Services
Drug safety, medical device, and laboratory regulation

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law may be called the Safe and Affordable Drugs from Canada Act of two thousand and twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Safe and Affordable Drugs from Canada
sections.text: This section adds a new part to existing federal drug laws. The government must create new rules within one hundred and eighty days that allow people to safely bring prescription drugs into the United States from Canada. People can only bring in drugs that meet certain requirements. The drug must be bought from a Canadian pharmacy that the government has approved. A Canadian pharmacist who has a license must fill the prescription. The person can only buy the drugs for their own use, not to sell to others. They can only get up to a ninety day supply at one time. The prescription must come from a doctor licensed in a United States state. The drug must have the same active medicine, be taken the same way, have the same form, and have the same strength as a drug already approved by the government in the United States. Some types of drugs cannot be brought in from Canada. These include controlled substances (drugs that have strict government rules because they can be dangerous or addictive), biological products (medicines made from living things), drugs that are put directly into the body through tubes or needles, drugs that are breathed in during surgery, drugs made through special biotechnology processes, drugs that must be kept cold, and drugs that react to light. To be an approved Canadian pharmacy, the pharmacy must be physically located in Canada. The government must certify that it has a license to operate and give out prescription drugs to people in Canada. The government will post a list on its website of all approved Canadian pharmacies, including their website addresses, where people can buy prescription drugs. To get certified as an approved pharmacy, the pharmacy must have been in business for at least five years before applying and must have a purpose other than just participating in this program. The pharmacy must follow the pharmacy rules and regulations of its Canadian province. The pharmacy must have systems in place to make sure it follows all laws and regulations and must have policies to keep checking that it stays in compliance. The pharmacy must participate in quality testing programs, including secret testing, to make sure their work is accurate and reliable. The pharmacy must agree to use laboratories approved by the government to test their products for safety and effectiveness. The pharmacy must have a way to handle complaints and must be held responsible if it breaks the rules. The pharmacy cannot resell products from online pharmacies outside Canada to customers in the United States. The pharmacy must meet any other requirements the government sets up.
